title: How Notre Dame Was Saved by Minutes
subtitle: 巴黎圣母院：生死十五分钟

paragraphs.en: Notre Dame Cathedral in the heart of Paris was within 15 to 30 minutes of complete destruction as firefighters battled to stop flames reaching its bell towers on Monday evening, French authorities have revealed. A greater disaster was averted by members of the Paris fire brigade, who risked their lives to remain inside the burning monument to create a wall of water between the raging fire and the two towers on the west of the building.
paragraphs.zh: 法国当局披露，周一晚间巴黎市中心的巴黎圣母院在消防员奋力阻止火焰蔓延至钟楼时，距完全毁灭仅有15到30分钟。巴黎消防队员冒着生命危险留在燃烧的建筑内，在烈焰与西侧两座塔楼之间筑起一道水幕，从而避免了一场更大的灾难。

paragraphs.en: The revelation of how close France came to losing its most famous cathedral emerged as police investigators questioned workers involved in the restoration to establish the cause of the devastating blaze. Paris prosecutor Remy Heitz said that an initial fire alert was sounded at 6:20 pm on Monday but no fire was found. The second alert was sounded at 6:43 pm, and the blaze was discovered on the roof.
paragraphs.zh: 警方调查人员询问参与修复工作的工人以确定火灾原因时，法国差一点失去最著名大教堂的内情被披露出来。巴黎检察官雷米·海茨表示，周一晚6点20分拉响了第一次火警，但未发现火情。6点43分第二次警报响起，屋顶发现火情。

paragraphs.en: More than €650 million was raised in a few hours on Tuesday as French business leaders and global corporations donated to a restoration campaign launched by President Emmanuel Macron. But as emergency services picked through the burnt debris, accusations resurfaced that the cathedral, immortalised in Victor Hugo's novel, was already crumbling before the fire.
paragraphs.zh: 周二数小时内便筹集了超过6.5亿欧元，法国商界领袖和跨国企业纷纷向马克龙总统发起的修复运动捐款。但当救援人员在烧焦的废墟中搜寻时，指责声再次浮现：这座因维克多·雨果小说而不朽的教堂在火灾前就已破败不堪。

words.meaning: v. 使不朽，使名垂千古

paragraphs.en: The cathedral is owned by the French state and has been at the centre of a years-long dispute over who should finance restoration of its collapsing staircases, crumbling statues and cracked walls. Jean-Michel Leniaud, president of the scientific council at the National Heritage Institute, said: What happened was bound to happen. The lack of adequate maintenance is the cause of this catastrophe.
paragraphs.zh: 该教堂归法国国家所有，多年来一直处于由谁出资修复坍塌楼梯、破损雕像和开裂墙壁的争议中心。国家遗产研究所科学委员会主席让-米歇尔·勒尼奥表示：“发生的事情是注定的。缺乏充分维护是这场灾难的根源。”

paragraphs.en: After the blaze was declared extinguished 15 hours later, interior minister Laurent Nunez said the structure had been saved but remained vulnerable. He praised the firefighters but admitted the cathedral's fate had been uncertain. They saved the main structure, but it all came down to 15 to 30 minutes, Nunez said.
paragraphs.zh: 火灾发生15小时后被宣布扑灭，内政部长洛朗·努内斯表示建筑结构虽已保住但仍很脆弱。他称赞了消防员，但承认教堂的命运曾悬于一线。努内斯说：“他们保住了主体结构，但一切都取决于那15到30分钟。”

words.meaning: adj. 脆弱的，易受伤害的

paragraphs.en: In a surprise televised address, Macron said he wanted the cathedral rebuilt within five years. Notre Dame is our history, our literature, the centre of our life, Macron said. This history is ours and so we will rebuild Notre Dame. It is what our history deserves.
paragraphs.zh: 在一次出人意料的电视讲话中，马克龙表示希望在五年内重建大教堂。马克龙说：“巴黎圣母院是我们的历史，我们的文学，我们生活的中心。这段历史属于我们，因此我们要重建巴黎圣母院。这是我们的历史应得的。”

paragraphs.en: The fire started at the base of the 93-metre spire at about 6:40 pm on Monday and spread through the roof, made up of hundreds of oak beams dating back to the 13th century. As hundreds of tourists and Parisians watched the spire catch fire and collapse, dramatic videos spread across social media, attracting emotional responses worldwide.
paragraphs.zh: 火灾于周一晚约6点40分从93米高的尖塔底部燃起，随后蔓延至由数百根可追溯至13世纪的橡木梁构成的屋顶。当数百名游客和巴黎市民目睹尖塔起火坍塌时，震撼的视频在社交媒体上迅速传播，引发了全球的情感共鸣。

paragraphs.en: The 500 firefighters battled to prevent flames from reaching the two main towers where the bells hang. If the towers' wooden frame had caught fire, the bells — the largest weighing 13 tons — could have crashed down, causing both towers to collapse. Police assessed the 850-year-old structure over the next 48 hours, identifying three main holes and extensive beam loss. Preliminary inspections suggested the three ornate stained glass rose windows survived. The culture minister said relics were held at the Hôtel de Ville and smoke-damaged artworks taken to the Louvre.
paragraphs.zh: 500名消防员奋力阻止火焰蔓延至悬挂大钟的两座主塔。若塔楼的木制框架着火，重达13吨的大钟可能坠落，导致双塔坍塌。警方在随后48小时内对这座850年历史的建筑进行了评估，确认了三处主要空洞和大量梁架损失。初步检查表明，三扇华丽的彩绘玫瑰花窗幸存。文化部长称圣物存放于市政厅，受烟熏损毁的艺术品已送往卢浮宫。

words.meaning: adj. 华丽的，装饰精美的

paragraphs.en: Sixteen copper statues decorating the spire had been removed for restoration just days before the fire. French commentators noted the fire had united the country where Macron had failed. Art historian Alexandre Gady said: We've been saying for years that the budget for maintaining historic monuments is too low. The prosecutor's office opened an inquiry into involuntary destruction by fire, indicating the cause was believed accidental.
paragraphs.zh: 装饰尖塔的16尊铜像在火灾前几天刚被拆除送去修复。法国评论人士指出，火灾在团结国家方面做到了马克龙未能做到的事。艺术史学家亚历山大·加迪表示：“多年来我们一直说维护历史古迹的预算太低。”检察官办公室已就“过失纵火”展开调查，表明火因被认为是意外。
